rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RENE D. CHEVALIER

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RENE D. CHEVALIER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/30/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Paragraph 4 of the petition does not appear to fully disclose all actions Administrator took under his IAEA authority. If there is only cash on hand, what happened to the securities? (2) A petition for final distribution must state the DATE on which notice was mailed to the FTB. [LRSF 14.35 I.2.] (3) The character of estate property is not that "it consists of the property of the decedent." The character of a decedent's property is whether it was separate or community property. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues (a supplement, verified by the administrator) to the assigned examiner by 12/16/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (A petitioner does not need to give notice to himself, but if notice had been required, it would have been defective, as the instructions for how to appear remotely were not attached. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] The attorney's declaration in support of the reimbursement of costs advanced were not supportive; the declaration merely repeated what was in the petition, when attorney costs should be fully itemized, including setting forth dates, payees, descriptions (each e-filing), and amounts.) NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.]

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Amended petition filed by Tiana Blunt is continued to 1/15/25. (No earlier date available.) No response to issues posted in the tentative ruling for the hearing on 9/16/24: (1) There was no "Legal Statement" attached to Attachment 1 to the amended petition. (2) Mailed notice must have attached to it the CURRENT instructions for how to make a remote appearance as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. (3) Published notice does not require these instructions to be included; however the wrong instructions were also published, so published notice is also incorrect. Deadline is 12/31/24 for providing courtesy copies to the assigned examiner. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ZETTIE BERNETT HIGHTOWER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Petition filed by Jennifer Hightower is continued to 1/15/25. (No earlier date available.) Incomplete response to issues posted for the hearing on 9/16/24. (1) Notice of Hearing must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) Items 2d and 3e of the petition are silent regarding bond. If the petitioner seeks full IAEA authority, bond will be for the full estimated value of the estate. (3) Item 5b of the petition must be answered. (4) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. Deadline is 12/31/24 for providing courtesy copies to the assigned examiner. A supplement to the petition, signed and verified by the petitioner, must be filed to address the missing information in her petition. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES D. BANKS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/27/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The narrative "proof of service" filed on 11/12/24 was not a proper proof of service of the AMENDED petition. If notice was mailed on 8/22/24, it could not have been for the amended petition, which was not e-filed until 9/16/24. (2) The only correct form of notice of a petition (or amended petition) to administer an estate is the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-121, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. If a pleading form of proof of service is filed, a copy of the DE-121 notice that was mailed must be attached to it. (3) Each notice must include a printout of the instructions for how to appear remotely. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) No proof of publication of the AMENDED petition has been filed. The strange proofs of publications filed on 11/12/24 were not the appropriate proofs of publication of the Notice of Amended Petition to Administer Estate. (5) Did the decedent's brothers Spencer Banks, Jr., and William Early Banks have any issue? (6) If the whereabouts of interested parties are unknown, a declaration must be filed describing efforts made to locate the persons entitled to notice. If, after diligent search, such persons cannot be located, an ex parte petition must be filed to request that notice to such persons be dispensed with. Deadline is 1/13/25 for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies. Courtesy copies to be provided: (1) proof of service of notice of the 1/27/25 hearing of the amended petition; (2) proof of publication of Notice of Amended Petition to Administer Estate; (3) a second supplement, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the petitioner, providing the information about the missing brothers. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (There is no need to narrate the fact that issues will be addressed; missing information should be provided in a supplement to the petition or correcting information should be provided in an amendment, and proofs of service or publication should just be filed. The odd pleading version of an order granting petition for appointment, etc., will be recycled.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE HARLAN SEE TRUST, DATED SEPTEMBER 11, 1990, AS RESTATED APRIL 14, 2010 AND AS AMENDED APRIL 11,
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For: 1. Order Compelling Trustee To Perform Duties Pursuant To Trust; 2. Breach Of Fiduciary Duties By Initial Successor Trustee; 3. Suspension Of Initial Successor Trustee Pending Removal; 4. Removal Of Initial Successor Trustee; 5. Attorney'S Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/06/2025 (no earlier date available), as the following issues (see tentative ruling for 09/25/2024 hearing) remain unaddressed: 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 30 days before the hearing. The DE-120 form filed on 10/30/2024 shows the date of mailing as 10/28/2024, less than 30 days before this hearing. New notice must be given. 2) The name and address of each beneficiary must be alleged in the petition. 3) The proof of service attached to the DE-120 forms did not list Beverly Johnson and some specific devisees removed by the first amendment. If any individual beneficiary is deceased, please provide his/her date of death. 4) Melinda (or Malinda) Randolph's consent to act as successor trustee is not on file. Please confirm the first name, as the operative trust documents refer to "Malinda", while the petition refers to "Melinda". 5) Please provide a description and market value of each asset currently held by the trust, together with estimated annual income therefrom, for the purpose of fixing the successor trustee's bond. 6) Please provide the settlor's date of death. 7) Please provide a copy of a trustee notification under Probate Code section 16061.7, if served by the respondent trustee. 8) The proposed order includes a finding that the respondent is deemed to have predeceased the settlor, but the petition and prayer for relief did not.
